summary: Updated PetProfileNav to accept two new props (healthActiveSection: string | null, onHealthSubNavClick: (sectionId: string) => void) and wired them into the sub-nav rendering. Sub-nav active class now checks isHealthActive && healthActiveSection === sub.hash instead of location.hash. Sub-nav click handler now calls onHealthSubNavClick when already on the health page, and falls back to navigate() for cross-page routing. useNavigate and useLocation are both retained. PetDetail.tsx already had the props wired at the call site (Task 1 completed that), so no changes were needed there.
notes_for_reviewer: PetDetail.tsx was already modified by Task 1 to pass healthActiveSection and onHealthSubNavClick props — confirmed at lines 247-248. No edits to PetDetail.tsx were required for this task.
TypeScript compilation returned no errors or warnings.
The active class guard (isHealthActive && ...) means sub-nav items will never show as active when on a different page, matching the spec's note about only showing active when on the health page.
